# LinkedIn Draft - 2026-06-13

Homebrew 6.0.0 introduces tap trust security, a new default JSON API, and Linux sandboxing.

Went through 40 links today. Here's what made the brief:

1. Homebrew 6.0.0
   Every Mac developer uses Homebrew. The tap trust model changes how you should think about third-party taps.

2. Anthropic apologizes for invisible Claude Fable guardrails
   Directly relevant to anyone building on Claude. Hidden guardrails affecting output quality is a trust issue for API consumers.

3. Hundreds of AUR packages attacked by infostealer
   Active supply chain attack affecting a major Linux package repository. Immediate action required for Arch users.

4. AI agent bankrupted their operator while trying to scan DN42
   Directly relevant to anyone running autonomous agents. Concrete cost-control lesson.

5. Zed introduces DeltaDB: tracking changes between commits
   Novel approach to dev tooling that could influence how editors and AI assistants understand code changes.

Full brief with all 40 researched links → https://thenightlylibrarian.com/briefs/2026-06-13
